description.content: Manual reference logins. The collection-level pre-request script invokes these automatically for each role on first run.
Logs in as Administrador. Stores admin_token.
Logs in as Solicitante. Stores requester_token and requester_user_id.
Logs in as N1 authorizer. Stores approver_token and n1_user_id.
Logs in as N2 authorizer. Stores approver_n2_token and n2_user_id.
Fetches the CSRF token. Required as 'x-csrf-token' header on every mutating request.
Permission catalog management. All endpoints require admin token with permission:read or permission:write.
Lists permissions in the catalog. Requires permission:read.
Filter to active permissions only
Creates a new permission. Requires permission:write. Returns 201 with permissionId.
Updates a permission. Requires permission:write.
Soft-deletes the permission (sets active=false). Requires permission:write.
Permission group management — bundles of permissions that can be assigned to roles or users.
Lists permission groups. Requires permission:read.
Creates a permission group. Requires permission_group:manage.
Adds permissions to a group. Requires permission_group:manage.
Returns the group with its assigned permissions. Requires permission:read.
Assign permissions and groups to roles.
Assigns one or more permissions to a role. Requires role:manage_permissions.
Removes a permission from a role. Requires role:manage_permissions. Returns 204.
Assign permission groups directly to a user and read effective permissions.
Assigns permission groups to a user. Requires user:manage_permissions.
Returns the user's effective permissions (role + groups + direct). Requires permission:read.
End-to-end approval flow: Solicitante creates a travel request, N1 approves, N2 approves, final status verified via GET.
Creates a travel request as the Solicitante. Requires travel_request:create. Stores the resulting request_id.

description.content:
Postman collection for Module 2 (Authorization & Workflow) endpoints.

Covers:
- Permissions catalog CRUD (/api/admin/permissions*)
- Permission groups CRUD and assignment (/api/admin/permission-groups*)
- Role and user permission assignment (/api/admin/roles/:roleId/..., /api/admin/users/:userId/...)
- Approval workflow end-to-end: create travel request (Solicitante) → authorize N1 → authorize N2 → verify final status
- Negative scenarios: decline without comment (expects 400), admin route as Solicitante (expects 403)

Tokens:
- {{admin_token}} for /api/admin/* requests (Administrador)
- {{requester_token}} for travel request creation and reads (Solicitante)
- {{approver_token}} for first-level authorization (N1)
- {{approver_n2_token}} for second-level authorization (N2)

The collection-level pre-request script logs in all four roles on first run, fetches the CSRF token, and caches everything in collection variables. Subsequent requests reuse the cached tokens.

Task: NT-016
Logs in as Administrador. Stores admin_token.
